mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms robust consumer demand and residential investment, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance p < 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 during early-cycle periods across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5 percent annually forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 2.5 percent correlate with positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of observed months over a 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero 20-day forward return in > 55% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects aggregate economic expansion, which directly increases corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals a severe supply-side contraction and imminent recession, triggering broad equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops greater than 1.0 percent monthly coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in > 50% of historical instances.
